So anyway, there's investment. And most people invest almost without thought in external marketing and are grudging and grumpy about investing anything in internal marketing. So every time this comes up, again, mostly because of the entitlement thinking, everybody thinks they should get their referrals for free. Everybody understands they gotta pay money to go get a new customer off the street, but they think that they ought to get their referrals for free. Well, it doesn't work that way. So if you actually are gonna build a machine, you're gonna make an investment in building it, and you're gonna make an investment in running it every single day, every single week, every single month. And then third is integration, meaning that, see, three eyes, it's a literature. I love how you did that. You spent hours coming up with that. It's just, it's all these years. So integration, right? So there's two, it's integrated into your business process. It's integrated into your culture, both customer and staff. So everybody gets it. We're really, we really are a referral-driven practice. And I get that. I see it, I hear it, I smell it.
